title: Fermion renormalized vertex functions, effective mass, and condensate in an external Yang-Mills gauge field
abstract: We investigate the renormalized fermion-gluon vertex, the fermion effective mass, and the fermion condensate when the fermion propagates in an external Yang-Mills gauge field. We use an exact Green's function for the Dirac operator in a non-Abelian plane-wave gauge field to construct the renormalized vertex function, calculate the on-shell fermion self-energy, and the background-dependent condensate. We consider both the background and operator fields in the axial gauge $k^{μ} \mathcal{A}_{μ}^{a}=0$, thereby preserving the gauge. Its applications to strong-field QCD and non-Abelian Schwinger physics are discussed.
summary: This paper extends the formalism of strong-field QED to non-Abelian Yang-Mills theory to study how gluonic backgrounds affect fermion properties. It demonstrates that such backgrounds induce a fermion condensate and a mass shift without altering the fundamental ultraviolet divergence structure of the theory.
main_problem: The paper investigates how a classical, periodic Yang-Mills (non-Abelian) plane-wave background field modifies the fermion-gluon vertex, the fermion effective mass, and the fermion condensate.
main_result: The study derives a gauge-consistent pole mass shift and shows that the background field induces a non-zero fermion condensate, while the UV divergence structure of the theory remains unchanged by the background.
method: The author uses an exact Green's function for the Dirac operator in a non-Abelian plane-wave background, employing the Mandelstam-Leibbrandt prescription for regularization and a weak-field expansion for loop corrections.
model_or_system: A system of fermions interacting with an external, monochromatic, non-Abelian (SU(N)) plane-wave gauge field moving along a light cone, treated within the axial gauge.
key_observables: Renormalized fermion-gluon vertex function, fermion effective mass shift, and the fermion condensate.
important_parameters: Gauge coupling constant (g), color factors (CF, CA), field intensity parameter (alpha), and the light-like vector (k).
main_assumptions: The background field is assumed to be a classical, periodic plane-wave solution, and the analysis relies on a weak-field expansion.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the non-Abelian plane-wave background and the axial gauge framework, proceeds to calculate the dressed fermion propagator and vertex functions using a weak-field expansion, then derives the one-loop mass shift and fermion condensate, and concludes with a discussion on UV behavior and physical applications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
